title: Sunipia arunachalense
taxonomy: Sunipia arunachalense is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Sunipia, which is a group of epiphytic orchids. The classification of Sunipia arunachalense places it within the subfamily Orchidoideae, one of the largest subfamilies of orchids. Further, it can be classified as a member of the tribe Gastrodieae, a group of orchids characterized by their parasitic or mycoheterotrophic habits. By this classification, Sunipia arunachalense is considered a rare and unique species of orchid.

Additionally, Sunipia arunachalense is believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a promising candidate for developing new medicines. intro: Sunipia arunachalense is a plant species native to the Arunachal Pradesh region in India, classified under the family Orchidaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, providing various health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Bioactive constituents of Sunipia arunachalense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic potential. Traditionally, herbal preparations made from Sunipia arunachalense include extracts and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
